Test ID: B107
Severity: LOW
Confidence: MEDIUM
CWE: CWE-259
File: .\.venv\Lib\site-packages\mysql\connector\abstracts.py
Line number: 2028
More info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
2027	    @abstractmethod
2028	    def cmd_change_user(
2029	        self,
2030	        username: str = "",
2031	        password: str = "",
2032	        database: str = "",
2033	        charset: Optional[int] = None,
2034	        password1: str = "",
2035	        password2: str = "",
2036	        password3: str = "",
2037	        oci_config_file: str = "",
2038	        oci_config_profile: str = "",
2039	    ) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
2040	        """Changes the current logged in user.
2041	
2042	        It also causes the specified database to become the default (current)
2043	        database. It is also possible to change the character set using the
2044	        charset argument. The character set passed during initial connection
2045	        is reused if no value of charset is passed via this method.
2046	
2047	        Args:
2048	            username: New account's username.
2049	            password: New account's password.
2050	            database: Database to become the default (current) database.
2051	            charset: Client charset (see [1]), only the lower 8-bits.
2052	            password1: New account's password factor 1 - it's used instead
2053	                       of `password` if set (higher precedence).
2054	            password2: New account's password factor 2.
2055	            password3: New account's password factor 3.
2056	            oci_config_file: OCI configuration file location (path-like string).
2057	            oci_config_profile: OCI configuration profile location (path-like string).
2058	
2059	        Returns:
2060	            ok_packet: Dictionary containing the OK packet information.
2061	
2062	        Examples:
2063	            ```
2064	            >>> cnx.cmd_change_user(username='', password='', database='', charset=33)
2065	            ```
2066	
2067	        References:
2068	            [1]: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/dev/mysql-server/latest/\
2069	                page_protocol_basic_character_set.html#a_protocol_character_set
2070	        """
2071

File: .\.venv\Lib\site-packages\mysql\connector\connection.py
Line number: 1026
More info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
1025	
1026	    def cmd_change_user(
1027	        self,
1028	        username: str = "",
1029	        password: str = "",
1030	        database: str = "",
1031	        charset: Optional[int] = None,
1032	        password1: str = "",
1033	        password2: str = "",
1034	        password3: str = "",
1035	        oci_config_file: str = "",
1036	        oci_config_profile: str = "",
1037	    ) -> Optional[OkPacketType]:
1038	        """Change the current logged in user
1039	
1040	        This method allows to change the current logged in user information.
1041	        The result is a dictionary with OK packet information.
1042	
1043	        Returns a dict()
1044	        """
1045	        # If charset isn't defined, we use the same charset ID defined previously,
1046	        # otherwise, we run a verification and update the charset ID.
1047	        if charset is not None:
1048	            if not isinstance(charset, int):
1049	                raise ValueError("charset must be an integer")
1050	            if charset < 0:
1051	                raise ValueError("charset should be either zero or a postive integer")
1052	            self._charset_id = charset
1053	
1054	        self._mfa_nfactor = 1
1055	        self._user = username
1056	        self._password = password
1057	        self._password1 = password1
1058	        self._password2 = password2
1059	        self._password3 = password3
1060	
1061	        if self._password1 and password != self._password1:
1062	            self._password = self._password1
1063	
1064	        self.handle_unread_result()
1065	
1066	        if self._compress:
1067	            raise NotSupportedError("Change user is not supported with compression")
1068	
1069	        if oci_config_file:
1070	            self._oci_config_file = oci_config_file
1071	
1072	        self._oci_config_profile = oci_config_profile
1073	
1074	        ok_pkt = self._authenticator.authenticate(
1075	            sock=self._socket,
1076	            handshake=self._handshake,
1077	            username=self._user,
1078	            password1=self._password,
1079	            password2=self._password2,
1080	            password3=self._password3,
1081	            database=database,
1082	            charset=self._charset_id,
1083	            client_flags=self._client_flags,
1084	            auth_plugin=self._auth_plugin,
1085	            auth_plugin_class=self._auth_plugin_class,
1086	            conn_attrs=self._conn_attrs,
1087	            is_change_user_request=True,
1088	            krb_service_principal=self._krb_service_principal,
1089	            oci_config_file=self._oci_config_file,
1090	            oci_config_profile=self._oci_config_profile,
1091	            webauthn_callback=self._webauthn_callback,
1092	        )
1093	
1094	        if not (self._client_flags & ClientFlag.CONNECT_WITH_DB) and database:
1095	            self.cmd_init_db(database)
1096	
1097	        self._post_connection()
1098	
1099	        # return ok_pkt
1100	        return self._handle_ok(ok_pkt)
1101

905	    def cmd_change_user(
906	        self,
907	        username: str = "",
908	        password: str = "",
909	        database: str = "",
910	        charset: Optional[int] = None,
911	        password1: str = "",
912	        password2: str = "",
913	        password3: str = "",
914	        oci_config_file: Optional[str] = None,
915	        oci_config_profile: Optional[str] = None,
916	    ) -> None:
917	        """Change the current logged in user"""
918	        try:
919	            self._cmysql.change_user(
920	                username,
921	                password,
922	                database,
923	                password1,
924	                password2,
925	                password3,
926	                oci_config_file,
927	                oci_config_profile,
928	            )
929	
930	        except MySQLInterfaceError as err:
931	            raise get_mysql_exception(
932	                msg=err.msg, errno=err.errno, sqlstate=err.sqlstate
933	            ) from err
934	
935	        # If charset isn't defined, we use the same charset ID defined previously,
936	        # otherwise, we run a verification and update the charset ID.
937	        if charset is not None:
938	            if not isinstance(charset, int):
939	                raise ValueError("charset must be an integer")
940	            if charset < 0:
941	                raise ValueError("charset should be either zero or a postive integer")

2655	
2656	def _parse_multi_options(options, split_token=','):
2657	    r"""Split and strip and discard empties.
2658	
2659	    Turns the following:
2660	
2661	    A,
2662	    B,
2663	
2664	    into ["A", "B"]
2665	    """
2666	    if options:
2667	        return [o.strip() for o in options.split(split_token) if o.strip()]
2668	    else:
2669	        return options

Test ID: B107
Severity: LOW
Confidence: MEDIUM
CWE: CWE-259
File: .\venv\Lib\site-packages\mysql\connector\abstracts.py
Line number: 2028
More info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
2027	    @abstractmethod
2028	    def cmd_change_user(
2029	        self,
2030	        username: str = "",
2031	        password: str = "",
2032	        database: str = "",
2033	        charset: Optional[int] = None,
2034	        password1: str = "",
2035	        password2: str = "",
2036	        password3: str = "",
2037	        oci_config_file: str = "",
2038	        oci_config_profile: str = "",
2039	    ) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
2040	        """Changes the current logged in user.
2041	
2042	        It also causes the specified database to become the default (current)
2043	        database. It is also possible to change the character set using the
2044	        charset argument. The character set passed during initial connection
2045	        is reused if no value of charset is passed via this method.
2046	
2047	        Args:
2048	            username: New account's username.
2049	            password: New account's password.
2050	            database: Database to become the default (current) database.
2051	            charset: Client charset (see [1]), only the lower 8-bits.
2052	            password1: New account's password factor 1 - it's used instead
2053	                       of `password` if set (higher precedence).
2054	            password2: New account's password factor 2.
2055	            password3: New account's password factor 3.
2056	            oci_config_file: OCI configuration file location (path-like string).
2057	            oci_config_profile: OCI configuration profile location (path-like string).
2058	
2059	        Returns:
2060	            ok_packet: Dictionary containing the OK packet information.
2061	
2062	        Examples:
2063	            ```
2064	            >>> cnx.cmd_change_user(username='', password='', database='', charset=33)
2065	            ```
2066	
2067	        References:
2068	            [1]: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/dev/mysql-server/latest/\
2069	                page_protocol_basic_character_set.html#a_protocol_character_set
2070	        """
2071

File: .\venv\Lib\site-packages\mysql\connector\connection.py
Line number: 1026
More info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
1025	
1026	    def cmd_change_user(
1027	        self,
1028	        username: str = "",
1029	        password: str = "",
1030	        database: str = "",
1031	        charset: Optional[int] = None,
1032	        password1: str = "",
1033	        password2: str = "",
1034	        password3: str = "",
1035	        oci_config_file: str = "",
1036	        oci_config_profile: str = "",
1037	    ) -> Optional[OkPacketType]:
1038	        """Change the current logged in user
1039	
1040	        This method allows to change the current logged in user information.
1041	        The result is a dictionary with OK packet information.
1042	
1043	        Returns a dict()
1044	        """
1045	        # If charset isn't defined, we use the same charset ID defined previously,
1046	        # otherwise, we run a verification and update the charset ID.
1047	        if charset is not None:
1048	            if not isinstance(charset, int):
1049	                raise ValueError("charset must be an integer")
1050	            if charset < 0:
1051	                raise ValueError("charset should be either zero or a postive integer")
1052	            self._charset_id = charset
1053	
1054	        self._mfa_nfactor = 1
1055	        self._user = username
1056	        self._password = password
1057	        self._password1 = password1
1058	        self._password2 = password2
1059	        self._password3 = password3
1060	
1061	        if self._password1 and password != self._password1:
1062	            self._password = self._password1
1063	
1064	        self.handle_unread_result()
1065	
1066	        if self._compress:
1067	            raise NotSupportedError("Change user is not supported with compression")
1068	
1069	        if oci_config_file:
1070	            self._oci_config_file = oci_config_file
1071	
1072	        self._oci_config_profile = oci_config_profile
1073	
1074	        ok_pkt = self._authenticator.authenticate(
1075	            sock=self._socket,
1076	            handshake=self._handshake,
1077	            username=self._user,
1078	            password1=self._password,
1079	            password2=self._password2,
1080	            password3=self._password3,
1081	            database=database,
1082	            charset=self._charset_id,
1083	            client_flags=self._client_flags,
1084	            auth_plugin=self._auth_plugin,
1085	            auth_plugin_class=self._auth_plugin_class,
1086	            conn_attrs=self._conn_attrs,
1087	            is_change_user_request=True,
1088	            krb_service_principal=self._krb_service_principal,
1089	            oci_config_file=self._oci_config_file,
1090	            oci_config_profile=self._oci_config_profile,
1091	            webauthn_callback=self._webauthn_callback,
1092	        )
1093	
1094	        if not (self._client_flags & ClientFlag.CONNECT_WITH_DB) and database:
1095	            self.cmd_init_db(database)
1096	
1097	        self._post_connection()
1098	
1099	        # return ok_pkt
1100	        return self._handle_ok(ok_pkt)
1101

941	                raise ValueError("charset should be either zero or a postive integer")
